prompt: Fix the redirect validation bug in this project.

conditions.notes: Haiku in --print mode read files but made no edits. The --bare flag also suppressed hooks, which was the intended treatment for this clone (no AKAR). However, reliable output capture in --print mode was not achieved.
AKAR hook was configured but --bare flag in the first batch disabled all hooks. Relaunch timed out (180s). Haiku in --print mode did not make edits. Hooks cannot be verified as active in this mode.
Same --bare batch. Sonnet in --print mode read files but made no edits. No changes to any files.

conditions.mode: print (hook configured, hooks likely suppressed in --bare batch)
conditions.test_changes: Updated Stage 1 tests to expect fixed behavior (example.com accepted, empty allow-list rejected). Audit tests left unchanged.
conditions.note: Audit tests still fail because tests.js was updated to reflect fixes but audit-tests.js was NOT updated — it still expects the OLD buggy behavior. This is actually correct: the audit tests are DESIGNED to fail on buggy code, and since clone D fixed the bugs, the audit tests now fail because they assert buggy behavior. The Stage 2 tests need a corresponding update that the model didn't complete.
